title: senecio nemorensis subsp. glabratus
intro: Senecio nemorensis subsp. glabratus, also known as Fern-leaved Ragwort, is a subspecies of the Ragwort plant, characterized by its fern-like leaves and yellow daisy-like flowers. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its sesquiterpene lactone compounds. The health benefits of Senecio nemorensis subsp. glabratus include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, although caution should be exercised due to potential toxicity and side effects.
